2605.07622.title: Is She Even Relevant? When BERT Ignores Explicit Gender Cues
2605.07622.paper_summary: Gender bias in large language models has primarily been investigated for English, while languages with grammatical or morphological gender remain comparatively understudied. This paper investigates how and when gender information emerges in a Dutch BERT model trained from scratch, offering one of the first checkpoint-level analyses of bias formation in a Transformer architecture for a language combining overt morphological gender marking and generic forms. By extracting contextual embeddings throughout training, we construct dynamic gender subspaces using linear SVMs to trace when gender becomes linearly encoded and how this encoding evolves over time. Contextual embeddings are often assumed to integrate contextual cues robustly, allowing models to adjust the representation of a word depending on its more local usage. We therefore test whether explicit gender cues in controlled sentence templates (e.g., Zij is een loodgieter ('She is a plumber')) can override learned statistical associations (plumber -> male). Our findings challenge this assumption: although gender becomes clearly linearly separable around epoch 20 and is distributed across multiple embedding dimensions, the model struggles to update its internal gender representation in light of explicit contextual cues in short sentence templates. Stereotypical gender-profession pairings are predicted far more accurately than anti-stereotypical ones, and generic forms in Dutch systematically default to a male interpretation, even when the context explicitly denotes a female referent. Together, our results seem to indicate that contextualization in the representations learned by our Dutch BERT model is not sufficiently dynamic along the probed gender direction: explicit gender cues in anti-stereotypical contexts are not reliably reflected in the resulting representations, resulting in persistent male-default behaviour.
2605.07622.paper_summary_zh: 性別偏見在大型語言模型中的研究主要集中於英語，而具有文法或形態性別的語言則相對較少受到研究。本文探討了在從零開始訓練的荷蘭BERT模型中，性別信息如何以及何時出現，提供了對於結合明顯形態性別標記和通用形式的語言中，Transformer架構偏見形成的首次檢查點級分析之一。通過在訓練過程中提取上下文嵌入，我們使用線性支持向量機構建動態性別子空間，以追蹤性別何時變得線性編碼以及這種編碼如何隨時間演變。上下文嵌入通常被認為能夠穩健地整合上下文線索，允許模型根據詞語的更局部用法調整其表示。因此，我們測試了在受控句子模板中（例如，Zij is een loodgieter（「她是一名水管工」））的明確性別線索是否能夠覆蓋學習到的統計關聯（plumber -> male）。我們的研究結果挑戰了這一假設：雖然性別在第20個世代左右變得明顯線性可分，並且分佈在多個嵌入維度上，但模型在短句模板中面對明確上下文線索時，卻難以更新其內部性別表示。刻板印象中的性別職業配對的預測準確性遠高於反刻板印象的配對，而荷蘭語中的通用形式系統性地默認為男性解釋，即使上下文明確指示女性參照。總的來說，我們的結果似乎表明，我們的荷蘭BERT模型所學習的表示在探測的性別方向上並不夠動態：在反刻板印象的上下文中，明確的性別線索並未可靠地反映在結果表示中，導致持續的男性默認行為。
2605.07622.author: Jonas Klein et.al.
2605.07622.authors: Jonas Klein, Chiara Manna, Eva Vanmassenhove
